heading: Reporting and data collection
label: 1727a Reporting and data collection
notes: References in TextThis subchapter, referred to in subsec. (d)(1), was in the original “this Act”, and was translated as reading “this title”, meaning title II of , known as the Public Lands Corps Act of 1993, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 91–378
Prior ProvisionsA prior was renumbered section 211 and is classified to .section 209 of Pub. L. 91–378section 1728 of this title

children.text: the number of participants enrolled in the Corps and the length of the term of service for each participant;
the projects carried out by Corps participants, categorized by type of project and Federal agency;
the total amount and sources of funding provided for the service of participants;
the type of service performed by participants and the impact and accomplishments of the service; and
any other similar data determined to be appropriate by the Chief Executive Officer of the Corporation for National and Community Service or the Secretaries.

children.heading: Data
children.label: b Data
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , and annually thereafter, the Secretaries shall submit to the Chief Executive Officer of the Corporation for National and Community Service the data described in subsection (a).March 12, 2019

children.heading: Data collection
children.label: c Data collection
children.text: The Chief Executive Officer of the Corporation for National and Community Service may coordinate with qualified youth or conservation corps to improve the collection of the required data described in subsection (a).

children.label: A
children.text: the data collection and reporting requirements of this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: implementing and issuing guidance on eligibility for noncompetitive hiring status under .section 1726(d) of this title

children.heading: Designation of coordinators
children.label: 2 Designation of coordinators
children.text: The Secretary shall designate a coordinator to coordinate and serve as the primary point of contact for any activity of the Corps carried out by the Secretary.
